reviews.review_text:
Metro 2033 Redux is one of the most atmospheric post-apocalyptic games ever made. The game throws you into the dark underground tunnels of Moscow after a nuclear war, where every bullet matters, the air is toxic, and deadly mutants can attack from anywhere.

The biggest strength of the game is its incredible atmosphere. The sound design, dark environments, gas mask mechanics, and constant feeling of danger make the world feel immersive and realistic. Artyom’s story is engaging and emotional, while the gameplay mixes stealth, horror, and action very well.

The Redux version improves the graphics, AI, lighting, and gameplay mechanics compared to the original release, making it the best way to experience the game. The survival elements and limited resources create a tense experience that keeps you focused from start to finish.

Some action sections can feel a bit clunky at times, but the exploration and horror moments easily make up for it. The game is also praised for being linear and story-driven without unnecessary open-world filler.

Overall, Metro 2033 Redux is an excellent FPS horror experience with a unique world, unforgettable atmosphere, and a strong story. If you enjoy immersive single-player games with survival and horror elements, this game is definitely worth playing.

Great game, the atmosphere was really good. It was dark, heavy and kinda scary, and there were some moments where I got scared a bit. The story was interesting, It maybe wasn’t very long, but it felt like enough, I didn’t have the feeling that something was missing.

The shooting was satisfying if you get a weapon that works well for you. You also need to be careful about how you use bullets. I got into moments where I was short on ammo and needed to loot as quickly as I could. :D

Overall, I would say it’s a great game and I recommend giving it a try!

Most post-apocalyptic games give you a feeling of “adventure”.
But Metro 2033 is not like that, it feels more like a suffocating long night train ride.
[h3] Dark, confined, terrifying, oppressive, endless. [/h3]

Most of the game’s story takes place deep inside the metro tunnels.
An underground world beneath the surface.
The environment is almost always pitch black.
You can barely see what’s ahead, and danger is everywhere — mutated creatures lurk in every tunnel.

The game fully captures the feeling of nuclear apocalypse.
Even when you reach the surface, there is no “breathing space”.
You are met with ruins, mutated creatures, and a nuclear winter that wraps everything in cold silence.

There is a very special system in this game:
[b] Bullets are currency. [/b]
Bullets come in two types:
1: Homemade standard rounds
2: Military-grade rounds left from the old world (which also function as money)
The second type is both ammunition and currency.

If you play in survival mode, resources become extremely tight.
I often ended up in situations where I simply ran out of bullets.
In that complete darkness, the tension becomes extremely intense.
Once your homemade ammo is gone, you can switch to military rounds, but when those are gone too, you lose both your firepower and your “buying power”, and can only rely on scavenging.

Weapon systems further strengthen this survival pressure.
Pneumatic weapons require manual pumping to maintain pressure.
Gas masks can break. Filter cartridges have time limits.
You are constantly “maintaining your own survival state”.

What’s more interesting is humanity itself.
Humanity destroyed the surface world with war, yet continues repeating history underground.
Different factions form inside metro stations, and eventually evolve into forms of “authoritarian metro regimes”, constantly fighting each other again.

The irony of history is:
[h3] The only lesson humanity learns from history is — that humanity never learns anything. [/h3]

What I love most is the atmosphere and storytelling rhythm.
There is no comfort.
No emotional rest point.
Only continuous pressure.

PS:
If the game feels too dark or too resource-starved, you can adjust the experience:
1: Play Spartan mode
2: Max out brightness settings
3: Let companions do most of the fighting (they are essentially immortal and have infinite ammo)
4: Focus on movement and avoidance instead of combat

But there is a trade-off.
The survival tension weakens significantly.
[h3] And the feeling of being a desperate survivor in a dying underground world fades with it. [/h3]

the absolute PEAK of gaming right here. dont even read the review just buy it you sausage.

2033 has the best atmosphere by a MILE.

from dark, cold n empty moscow to stations of fire, laughter n водка.

the sound engine was developed specifically for 2033 and it shows. i have not once in 100s of games heard a game sound so accurate, so real and overwhelming, gunshots recorded in ukranian underground tunnels, footsteps perfectly matching spatial perception. 2014 graphics become irrelevant when you understand how well the atmosphere and sound synergise.

the combat is nothing special in all honesty, basic 1st person shooter although decent weapon choice and customisation, but the story is where this game really shines.

the world is ending, theres only room for survival, the game forces you to choose between morals yet gives you the option to choose the type of person you want to be. the future seems overwhelmingly bleak, yet you can still find moments of laughter, drinking and reminiscing with your fellow russian comrades. 

its not just the story thats bleak, its the pressure the game constantly puts you under, reminding you that you're in a time of scarcity, every bullet is a choice – spend it on an enemy or save it to buy filters for your gas mask, either way, you're always running out of something.

as a polish man i dont think 4A games could have executed the eastern european / soviet atmosphere any better than they did, if your slavic you will appreciate this game more than most.

played at 1440p low settings 60-120fps consistently on a 27 inch monitor, powered by rog ally, zero stuttering or performance issues.

flawless execution of a concept, even if not 100% polished. 9.6/10.
The legendary first installment of the Metro series. It's perfect, and the plot is a masterpiece.

This game sucks. I couldn't even fu*k Miller.

Nah, but for real, this was a fantastic masterpiece. I absolutely loved the story. It was entertaining, incredible, and exceptionally well written. The monsters were extremely well designed, both in terms of their sounds and animations. The weapons were incredibly satisfying to use and fire, especially because of the sound design and atmosphere, which this game absolutely nails.

The main character is well written, including the NPCs. The environments are fantastic and really immersive. If this game had come out in 2026, I probably would have given it a 5/10, but for a game released in 2014, it's peak gaming.

I can't wait to play the other games in this series.
Metro games are too much fun sometimes. This game had a good story and good combat. I had played this a lot on Xbox before transitioning to pc. I'd recommend you pick it up and try it out.

Always a good playthrough if you like post apocalyptic settings.
If you like a challenge try ranger hardcore

An overwhelming novelize first in the series title. I quite enjoy playing it. Also, one of the best post apocalyptic game.
- Literally, librarians and the demons are pretty tough to beat, made me sweat around 3 mins max to kill a Librarian, PSST.... but I didn't know, a flame grenade can do the job in 5 secs, LOL!

100% Recommended if you are a fan of post apocalyptic style setting. SO, GO FOR IT!

so far I'm very impressed with this one. For a 2014 game it holds up very well. This is what happens when game designers pour their heart into something.

The story is very interesting set in Moscow in the aftermath of a devastated apocalyptic world. As you can imagine ♥♥♥♥ is ♥♥♥♥♥♥. Rival wars going on all while mutated freakish monsters are attacking everything.

The gunplay is very satisfying with a nice touch of fear coupled with the unsettling feeling of never being fully safe. Even in the silence the shadows play tricks with your eyes. Every sound startles your nerves. They've really put a lot of detail into this game.

The characters are solid as you naturally form a bond or take a liking to them almost as if you're worried for their safety. The dialogue and voice overs are a nice quality and believable. Sound quality in general is pretty fantastic.

Everything is so dark in the underground I hate it in the best way. Fighting the monsters proves to be more of a challenge than I anticipated. Thinking it would feel safer on the surface in the light is huge mistake. I don't know what's worse. Basically pick your poison at this point. Trying to keep a stock of charcoal filters for your gas mask is a nightmare and always has you in panic mode as you try not to waste precious time.

As if threats on the ground weren't enough don't forget to check the skies.

I'm extremely happy with my purchase as it's well worth full price. I can't wait to continue the series and play the next two chapters I'm giving this one 10/10 sick work.

“Claustrophobic, brutal, and unforgettable.”

Metro 2033 Redux is one of those games that immediately pulls you into its world and refuses to let go. Set in the ruins of a post-apocalyptic Moscow, it trades power fantasy for constant tension, scarcity, and survival.

From the very beginning, the atmosphere does most of the heavy lifting. Dark tunnels, flickering lights, gas masks running low on filters—it all creates a sense of pressure that never really goes away. You’re not just fighting enemies; you’re fighting the environment itself.

Combat is slow, deliberate, and punishing. Every bullet matters, and rushing in usually means death. This makes every encounter feel meaningful, even when things get chaotic. The game rewards patience and awareness rather than aggression.

The Redux version improves visuals and performance, making the already strong atmosphere even more immersive. Lighting, textures, and sound design all work together to build a world that feels genuinely oppressive and alive at the same time.

The story is dark, philosophical, and surprisingly emotional in places. It’s less about saving the world and more about surviving it—and what that does to people.

Of course, it’s not perfect. AI can be inconsistent, and the gameplay loop may feel a bit rigid for players expecting modern shooter freedom. But that rigidity is also part of its identity.

Pros:

Incredible atmosphere and immersion
Strong survival-focused gameplay
Great sound and environmental design
Improved visuals in Redux version

Cons:

Linear and sometimes rigid gameplay
AI inconsistencies
Can feel slow for action-focused players

Verdict:
If you want a survival FPS that prioritizes atmosphere and tension over constant action, Metro 2033 Redux is an essential experience.

Recommended 👍
One of my most favorite series, this game is just amazing. Weakest from the rest of the series imo, but still amazing game.

Decided to go through the series again when I saw the news that a new game was coming out.  I originally played Metro 2033 on the Xbox 360 around the time the game came out.  It was very different from any other first-person shooter I had played before.  The game is linear, but it creates a mood and atmosphere that lots of other games fail to achieve.  This Redux edition was a great way to hop back in.  It's not a perfect game, but it is still worth playing today.  The major flaw that I found with this game was when you are placed in a position to rely on your ally AI.  Throughout the game you are placed in a handful of situations where you are fighting alongside companions.  The only problem is is that they are very braindead.  What makes matters worse is that the enemy AI is almost entirely focused on you.  These moments create a good amount of frustration as you watch the enemies easily walk right past your allies and then proceed to clobber you with everything they've got.  There aren't enough of these moments to completely render the game unplayable, but they definitely bring down an otherwise great experience.  If you are in the mood for a good, claustrophobic survival horror experience, you could definitely do worse.
its straightforward and really good story game, i heard about glitches in the game happening but none happened to me
Metro 2033, though it's quite outdated, is absolutely one of the best post-apocalyptic single player games I have ever played, abbsolute cinema.
good game if you are a fan of the books....and the librarians that took care of them.
playing through the games again to get ready for 2039 and this game is still as good as it was back in the day
If you play this game, remember that librarians are very friendly but do not provoke them.

100% completed.

Came for the post-apocalyptic shooter, stayed for the atmosphere. Metro 2033 isn't perfect, the AI and gunplay are sometimes annoying but the world, tension and immersion are great 10/10. The dark tunnels, ruined surface and constant struggle for resources make every journey feel tense and memorable. Great introduction for the second game as well.
Some grindy achievements, but hey...
Recommended.
the level design is one of the worst i have played in any game but the atmosphere and the story are amazing one of the best games i have played recently

The game introduces you to Artyom and he guides you through what humanity has become and not much more.  The focus is the world and it believable.  I believe in this nightmare at no point did i feel like i was playing in a made up world it felt like a story at a campfire a tale from people trying to survive at all cost.

Surprisingly, this game rewards you if you play in a more human/pacifist style...

The pros : Great lore , great atmosphere , good graphic . The cons : The gunplay is a little bit outdate , but enjoyable and the game still lack of instructions at some point , but overall , it's a great game , solid 8.5/10 => Would recommend
the atmosphere in this game is great, the gunplay is okay, but i like the stealth and just throwing knives at everyone :)

A genuinely unforgettable experience. Everything about this game is wonderful. I know there are differences from the book and the game, so am going to read through 2033 - 2035 before I continue with this series. This game is incredible

Super fun, can't believe it took me this many years to complete it. I played through about halfway when it originally came out and just never finished it. Finally with the announcement of the new game I decided to play through all of them. Definitely recommend.
Verry ATMOSPHERIC Game, Good story, gameplay and graphics, despite being an older game. I Have enjoyed it more than Metro Last light.

An absolute masterpiece of atmospheric storytelling. A must-play survival horror!

I have replayed Metro 2033 Redux multiple times, and it never fails to blow me away. The Redux version is exactly how this game was meant to be experienced. The graphical overhaul, smooth performance, and improved gameplay mechanics taken from Last Light make this linear, story-driven shooter feel modern and incredibly polished.

The atmosphere in this game is unmatched. Walking through the dark, claustrophobic tunnels of the Moscow metro with nothing but a flashlight and a lighter creates a constant sense of tension. The survival elements are brilliant—constantly checking your watch for air filter time, wiping blood and condensation off your gas mask, and using military-grade ammo as actual currency makes every decision feel impactful.

The story is deeply engaging, combining gritty post-apocalyptic realism with supernatural, philosophical elements. It shifts perfectly between terrifying stealth sections, desperate defenses against mutant hordes, and breathtaking, dangerous segments on the frozen surface of ruined Moscow.

If you love immersive, story-heavy shooters with a thick, survival-horror atmosphere, Metro 2033 Redux is one of the best games ever made.
One of the best games story-wise that exist around. Although it's the first installment and the mechanism-graphics aren't as good as the next games the immersion is still amazing

i beat it for the first timeand i say its a 9/10 it starts slow and its surprisingly short for what i expected either way the ending is epic and id say if you like horror and a challenge get this it has visible bugs with graphics and rendering tho

Good survival horror game. The story is fairly convincing - an apocalyptic atmosphere with factions struggling to make a home and enforce order in the underground of Moscow. It can be cheesy at times, however, in that regard. Mutant abominations await on the surface for those daring enough to explore, which you, as the protagonist, are.

I suggest Metro 2033 Redux for every fan of either survival or horror post-apocalyptic stories. The game is a perfect cocktail of both; it offers a dark and strong atmosphere with captivating gameplay and a world that feels threatening and believable. While the game may not suit people who like constant action, story lovers will probably think it’s a good option.

I don't have many complaints about the game, however I wholeheartedly believe that the story was drawn out a bit towards the end of the game.

In conclusion, storyline, gritty atmosphere, and the modest arsenal of weapons to choose from, made the game immersive. I would definitely recommend picking this up and playing the storyline at least once.
its very fun theres some bits in it where you die like 5 times but when you complete it its like so exciting

Such a cool shooter! For the 2014 game it still plays perfectly: cool graphics, crazy vibe, interesting side characters and such a cool mc! There are also mechanics such as a weapon upgrades, resource management(your money is literally your bullets you can fire up), aaand pretty... bizarre notes collection, that got me replay a few chapters. either way - it's worth it. 
runs on a steamdeck perfectly as well!
Fantastic game. I've played the original Metro 2033 a couple of times. Especially now with the announcement of Metro 2039.

This is one of those games where the atmosphere does half the work before you even fire a shot. The tunnels feel cold, dirty, crowded, dangerous, and somehow alive. Every station feels like people are barely holding things together with tape, old rifles, bad news, and whatever soup they’re pretending is dinner.

I love how the game makes survival feel personal. Ammo matters, filters matter, light matters, and going outside feels like a terrible idea that you still have to do anyway. The gas mask alone adds so much tension. Nothing says “relaxing evening” like checking your filter while something ugly screams at you in the dark.

The shooting and stealth both fit the world well. It’s not a superhero power trip. You feel vulnerable, under-equipped, and very aware that wasting bullets is basically financial self-harm in tunnel society. That makes every fight more tense, and every quiet moment more important.

Redux also makes the whole thing smoother and better-looking without losing what made it special. It’s dark, immersive, scary in the right places, and full of that heavy post-apocalyptic mood Metro does so well. Great game if you want a shooter with real atmosphere instead of just another hallway full of targets.

love this game waiting on the book to come exited to read it after playing this one great game 10/10 great story yes there's sum cut content cuz of the redux verson but still a very good story will be going for the good ending soon after finishing Metro exodus

I have bought all the games from the metro series, which just started with last light. But then i decided to play in order and man was that a good decision. Wonderful play style and dense atmosphere making one feel claustrophobic and the narration and mechanics of the game are really great. also works pretty well on my potato laptop, in most places it has around 1080p low/medium @ 30fps, in open world use 900p medium to have a stable 30 fps as 1080p even at low dips to 25-28 fps. Overall a very good game, easily a 8/10. Some part and mechanics are tricky and have u struck and frustrated for a while but once u look it up u can usually sort it out. Also a simple tip for anyone using pneumatic weapons or the flamethrower that u get towards the end game : if on pc/laptop press R(reload button) and then the shoot button(LMB) to repressurize it as it wont work otherwise, even with sufficient ammo.

[h1]👍 Recommended[/h1]
[b]44 hours on record[/b]

This was my first Metro game.

I've seen people praise the series for years, but never got around to playing it. Going in blind, I expected another post-apocalyptic shooter. What I got was one of the most atmospheric games I've played in a long time.

What stuck with me wasn't the combat or the mutants. It was the feeling of living in the Metro itself. Every station feels like a small community trying to survive after the world ended, and that makes the world incredibly easy to get invested in.

The game does tension better than most horror games. Running low on filters, conserving ammo, and hearing strange noises in the tunnels kept me on edge even when nothing was happening.

The gunplay is solid, the story kept me interested, and the world-building is easily the strongest part of the experience.

It's not perfect. The AI can be a little odd at times, stealth can feel inconsistent, and some encounters drag on longer than they should. But none of that was enough to pull me out of the experience.

What surprised me most is how well this game has aged. Even today, its atmosphere feels better than a lot of modern releases.

Now I understand why people speak so highly of the Metro series.

If you've somehow missed it like I did, give it a chance.

Just remember:

You're not the strongest thing in these tunnels.

And the game never lets you forget it.
What a beautiful game, I finished my first playthrough with only six bullets left. What a conclusion to this post apocalyptic story

Great story 
Great gameplay
Great horror
I would highly recommend this game to anyone looking for a single player game.
I got side tracked all lot due to the levels of the game with me constantly wanted to explore every location which lead to me finding secrets such as red keys for safes to open for more tools and currency to use in the world of metro. This is a great start to a franchise based on the books and look forward to playing the other games 9.5/10 - Game did crash a couple times

So I decided to give the Metro series a try after being tempted many times in the past. Glad I did.
Started it off with Metro 2033 and although its age , you can't tell most of the time that we are talking about a 2010 game (2014 Redux). Great story , likeable characters and an overall good experience playing this making me look forward for the rest of the series.

A very good game with a well-written story, memorable characters, and an atmosphere that few post-apocalyptic games manage to match. Metro 2033 Redux does an excellent job of immersing you in its dark and dangerous world, making every journey through the tunnels and the surface feel tense and meaningful.

The gunplay is solid, and while some weapons definitely feel better than others, I had a lot of fun experimenting with different loadouts throughout the game. There are also some nice easter eggs scattered around for fans of other Valve classics, including references to Half-Life and Left 4 Dead, which were fun to stumble across.

Despite the game's age, it still looks and plays surprisingly well. However, I did run into some strange freezing issues near the end of the game. I'm not entirely sure if this was caused by the game itself or some weird interaction with newer hardware, but thankfully it wasn't common enough to ruin the experience.

The story isn't overly long either, which I actually appreciated. It took me around 7 hours to complete, making it easy to finish without the game overstaying its welcome. Those 7 hours were packed with memorable moments, great atmosphere, and plenty of reasons to keep pushing forward to see what happened next.

Overall, Metro 2033 Redux was a fantastic experience that I thoroughly enjoyed. Now it's time to move on to Metro: Last Light Redux and hopefully work toward 100% completion before eventually diving into Metro Exodus. If the rest of the series is anything like this game, I'm in for a great time.
Great gameplay and story, very few bugs, highly recommend
the lore is intresting and the gameplay is great

Runs well, Crashed only once, great gameplay. Rough around the edges but great overall
8/10
A classic, simple elegant. One of personal all time favorites, if you are in to post apocalyptic games Metro series is a must!
The game is so good that i play it continuously and end the game in only 3 days lol, this game is also good at building up the atmosphere, it's a FPS game but sometime i thought it's a horror game
Great interactive world with an interesting story, i enjoyed learning about the metro and world.
A very good game - immersive, engaging, and a real gem for gamers.
I like a lot about Metro's immersion, gameplay and story! I feel glad to have finally played it from my backlog!

An OK shooter that takes you through a series of linear levels. It does a good job of providing a lot of combat, varied enemies, interesting weapons, and straightforward controls. The story is good; based on a book and benefits from that. But the designers smartly keep things at a fast pace by minimizing dialog. Performance is very good, 300 FPS according to AMD Adrenaline. The game is short and the human AI is subpar but for $1.99 USD (the current sale price) it's worth it.

On my computer I'd get intermittent hitching ever few seconds with vsync off, and about once a minute with vsync on. This isn't a very widespread issue from what I can tell but I never found a way to fix it.

great game, really beautiful visuals and gameplay. there is one gamebreaking bug at the end however which i had a lot of issues with. if you die before you reach the second checkpoint on the final chapter you will be softlocked in and cant go any further than the shelter. i had to quit to the main menu and start again from the chapters menu.
Solid 8/10 story with a 10/10 world design and atmosphere, The gunplay feels good and there are a handful of fun unique weapon designs that im a fan of.

Метро 2033 — отличная короткая атмосферная игра, которая не пытается растянуть себя на десятки часов и от этого только выигрывает.

Лучшее, что есть в игре — это уровни с людьми. Вооружившись ВСВ с глушителем и прибором ночного видения, я превратил игру в настоящий стелс-шутер: аккуратно обходил посты, ставил хэдшоты и зачищал вражеские базы практически бесшумно. Именно в такие моменты Метро раскрывается лучше всего.

Атмосфера тоже работает. Постапокалиптическое метро выглядит живым, станции наполнены разговорами и мелкими деталями, а вылазки на поверхность приятно разбавляют бесконечные тоннели. Не потому что там происходит что-то невероятное, а просто потому что после нескольких часов под землёй приятно увидеть небо, снег и руины Москвы.

А вот с монстрами у меня отношения не сложились. Большинство боёв с мутантами воспринимаются не как интересное испытание, а как проверка запасов патронов. Особенно это заметно в библиотеке. Библиотекари впечатляют внешне, но после очередного магазина, выпущенного в одну обезьяну размером с холодильник, восторг быстро сменяется вопросом: «А патроны мне потом кто вернёт?». В итоге последние уровни я буквально доедал на остатках боезапаса, а ближе к финалу просто начал заряжать военные патроны и стрелять направо и налево, потому что копить их дальше уже не было смысла.

Отдельно удивило, насколько слабо игра раскрывает собственный мир. Фанаты часто говорят о глубоких фракциях, политике и устройстве метро, но во время прохождения этого почти не ощущается. Красные, фашисты, спартанцы и жители станций в основном запоминаются разными моделями персонажей и репликами. Возможно, книги раскрывают этот мир гораздо лучше, но сама игра делает на этом довольно слабый акцент.

Не помог и главный герой. Артём ощущается скорее проводником по миру Метро, чем полноценным персонажем. Обычный парень со станции внезапно становится человеком, который проходит через орды мутантов, армии врагов и в итоге влияет на судьбу всего метро. За время прохождения я так и не смог к нему привязаться — куда больше запомнились оружие, локации и отдельные игровые ситуации.

В итоге Метро 2033 оставила после себя очень приятное впечатление. Это не глубокая ролевая история и не выдающийся сюжетный шутер, зато это компактное, атмосферное приключение на 8–10 часов с отличным оружием, хорошим стелсом и уникальным миром. Иногда этого более чем достаточно.

Had a great time playing the game. It was an amazing experience from start to finish. Story is good, sometimes you have to figure things out by yourself. 
The only time I had an issue was at the last chapter where my laptop crashed and blacked out 2 times at the same spot. IDK why tho.....
other than that it' a very  great game, nice atmosphere, great journey and definately worth playing.
Pretty good for a "reboot".  The graphics look great. it runs pretty well (aside from a few simple crash fixes"
I played it in 2026 and still worth it! Really good story, Atmosphere and  graphics even you can run it in you Budget PC.

I just want to say, the graphics, the story, Miller and so much more are peak. Im suprised Metro isint more popular, It is one of the best Story mode games I have genneraly ever played, I am rooting for the develepors to make 2039 and even then, I will still miss artoym

Although disliking anything to do with Russia due to Ukraine, looking for more similar to FO4 & FO76 game play which according to Youtube vids this has numerous similarities. It looks sounds great. I not very hard core so need help like WAND to not get frustrated fed up. Instead of stressing shoot-fests i prefer to take my time admiring this & that in scenery, weather, dialoque trees options with others. Companion helper like FO4 would add much to it. Came back to this yr after trying. Seems keys aren't working. Maybe due to WAND. I'll test without it. That wasn't the cause. At start of continue it flashed upper left an update available go to *** but that disappeared fast so dunno about that. Forum here has many bad issues caused by that shaders update so don't risk it. Solved frozen on spot by using end task since game keys stopped responding. Thanks to forum posters that said best instead of continue, use mission select. That worked when continue didn't, to laod earlier in that mission & proceed to sticking spot which I didn't stick to again. Continuing on it gets difficult & seemingly impossible even with WAND infinite health as sometimes those dammed tunnel creatures manage to kill ya. I gonna likely gonna have to give up on this dreadfully annoying game. BE WARNED.

A great game. Recently bought in steam after my first playthrough. The atmosphere and environment of this game is well designed. Highly recommended for people who like linear first person survival shooter or haven't tried this genre before.
If you are into stealth and post-apocalyptic story set in Moscow where you fight both the commies and nazis. Then this game will be interesting for you. I definitely recommend this game.

Absolutely stellar 1st entry into a franchise made as a love letter to STALKER and the like that brings its own identity to the table. Platforming sections are mildly frustrating but doesn't detract from the experience at all. 100% worth the money even when not on sale.

This game has held up surprisingly well. The art design and overall visuals are amazing. Also works great on Linux.

9/10 - Would try to survive the apocalypse again.

[b]Awesome game! [/b]
 I'm replaying it rn after finally reading the first book. The overarching story line is obviously the same, but there are quite a lot of surprisingly big differences. Keeps it really interesting, even if you've already read/played the other. 

 From my original playthrough where I also went for all the achievements I remember that some of the achievements were quite annoying to get because either the description is misleading or it's just REALLY finicky. 

 Also annoyingly the native Linux build is completely worthless as it's stuck in a tiny window and partially off-screen. Even with mods to try and fix it, you can then move the window but still cannot change the resolution. It just straight up ignores the config file. So, if you're planning on playing it on Linux just use the windows build through proton, that works perfectly fine. :3

This game has so many issues...crashes after crashes...i have been trying for the last 4h with many different solutions - doenst work. Game worked fine for the first half - now it crashes after every loading screen and i cant continue. Loved the original one. (and yes i have a very good PC)

This is the first game IT ALSO looks better, Ithan any modern game and it runs without frying your pc STORY IS AMAZING, GAMEPLAY AMAZING AND EVEN THE ACHIEVEMENTS ARE AMAZING

This is the PERFECT GAME
Great game, shame i didn't get the good ending tho, died way to many times, loved some of the infiltration aspects of it and the lore was very in-depth.

Metro 2033 on PC is a masterpiece of atmosphere, tension, and “why is everything trying to kill me.”  
It’s immersive, terrifying, and unforgettable — the perfect blend of horror, survival, and janky charm.
If you want a game that punches you in the soul and then hands you a flashlight with low battery, this is it.

I came into this game without knowing a thing, just knew that alot of people enjoyed it and it happen to be on sale. I can safely say this game is a hit. It's old but it has so much passion and love put into it. The audio work is so crisp I can still hear the filter low noise even after 100%ing. I look forward to seeing how the next games compare. 10/10

I've been trying to get into this game ever since it originally came out on the 360 and after all this time I think it's time to throw the towel and accept that I don't actually like this game that much. 

The atmosphere, the premise, the concepts are all really great aspects of this game; from the Idea behind the Black creatures, ammo as currency and just the whole idea behind living in Metros is really cool...

...but I don't like the actual game. 

It's corridor after corridor without any actual gameplay. It's an "experience" rather than an actual game. You are constantly atmospherically talking with comrades, looking through stuff and staying on the wagon of this atraction. I don't like it. 

Maybe Exodus is better suited for me since that one is apparently more "open" with wider sections to explore but Metro 2033 sadly just isn't fun to play. 

I'll check out the books for the story though, still really really like the whole idea behind this franchise.

I take the time about once or twice a year to replay all of the Metro games, starting with this one. I've 100% completed 2033 and Last Light, and well on my way to getting all the achievements in Exodus. 4A Games is who GSC wishes they were.

Don't wait for a sale, it's well worth the base price.
A very enjoyable game but there are the usual issues to be expected from a game this old.

I enjoyed this game, picked up the series on sale, had never played it before. It can be a little frustrating at times- figuring out where to go, sometimes you are literally "on rails" but is not too long, which is a bonus for me.

A very rich story and fun gameplay, Would recommend if you like post apocalyptic stories.

Would recommend all of the METRO games.
I set all the settings to Russian to force myself to learn the language while playing it. Man I'm having fun

This is a masterpiece of a game. I will be buying the rest of the series just to see more of the metro. A little confusing at times on where to go or how to interact properly. This game was made with so much passion and love and it shows.
Metro 2033 is one of the best games i've played. The whole world feels alive in its own dystopian way. You'll rarely find a game so immersive like this. It is worth any cent.
The game has a certain charm to it and the story compelling enough, that it overcomes the occasional bug and crash that you'll experience.

First time playing although I've had these games for years. Really wish id have played them along time ago. Good story, great visuals and although it typically feels like games I've played before there's something different and refreshing about Metro. I'm genuinely looking forward to playing Last Light, Exodus and definitely 2039. Solid 8/10

Banger hra, docela nadčasová na svůj rok vydání. Grafika je pěkná, gameplay je fajnovka a příběh je lambáda. Má to pár chybek jako dost starých her, hra crashne v Dead City pokud máte grafiku na něco jiného než low a pokud máte zaplý tesselation a ve vzácných případech když vás vezme demon tak vás může zhodit někam kde vás to bugne, říkám vzácně protože se mi stalo jenom jednou a nejspíš se to většině hráčům ani nestane ale ani to nevadí protože checkpointy jsou dobré takže si dáte reload last checkpoint a nehodí vás to moc zpátky. Velice dobrá hra doporučuju zahrát.

While not as good as Half Life, I thought this was a great game. Very interesting worldbuilding, creative weapons, good story (not impressive, but still enough to carry the game).

It's also a fun and very doable achievement hunt, as long as you do the stealth related ones on a separate run.

However, I wish the game had a more clear separation between gameplay and cutscenes which gets pretty frustrating and confusing. There's this one gameplay section where you get pushed backwards that I assumed was a cutscene so I died. Maybe some black bars to change the aspect ratio, or having the UI stay on during gameplay and disappear during cutscenes; just something to make it clear.

Also, the game would've benefited from having some design rules around what interactables should look like. I often got lost because the fake and real doors look the same. I know that interactables do subtly flash, but I think having different designs for them would be even better.

Playing the Metro franchise for the first time has been an experience like no other. The vibe, graphics, and art direction are timeless—they will never feel outdated, I’m certain of that. The first-person perspective makes the experience incredibly immersive, and the companions I’ve met along the journey ensured I never felt alone while navigating such a brutal world. I’d give Metro 2033 Redux a 9/10, and I’m very excited to dive into the next chapters.
Good amount of gameplay, cool thematic backdrop, nice visuals, good sound track. I very much enjoyed this game. I'm glad they are making another game to resolve some more of the plotlines.
Pretty fun game. Great to play especially if you are story-driven. Heard the book is really good as well.

When i got my PC i came to know about this game, at first i didn't know what was the hype about. Why people pleased this game so much. But when i played it for the first time after epic gave it away for free. This one and last light. I really got immersed in it. The world feels very interactive and lively, there's so much hidden things which you will miss if you rush this game. And without a doubt metro become my favourite franchise. I bought it on steam and replayed again. Currently on Last light. I am going every corner, hearing every conversations. I mean i am playing this game as intended. I will replay it for the third time as well. The whole franchise. I just love this game.
Although the game runs below 1920x1080 - Still an incredibly fun game and lots of side quests free roam and markets to explore 10/10!!!

A nice shooter road trip in a post-apocalyptic Moscow. 7/10.

Well, I think this game is a bit better when you have read the book. I never did. Since most factions in this world are just there and the only way to interact with them is to shoot them with a gun, I think this game would have huge potential if it was open world.
(I think Exodus is more open. I would not know; I have not played it yet.)

The story can be vague at times, and sometimes it can be confusing where you need to go. (I played this game without the HUD; I still enjoyed it.)

While the money system is cool, I never found myself using the good bullets as ammo (sometimes just by accident), but it's a cool concept. I'm just disappointed that it's just that: a cool concept, as you can find plenty of ammo.

Still a good recommendation if you love shooters. You should have no trouble running it on your PC/laptop.

Amazing game, a genuine story, engaging environment. The challenges are not designed to make you suffer but to help you really enjoy the struggle unlike many of the recent FPS games. "Old wood best to burn, old wine to drink, old friends to trust, and old authors to read." 
Worth every penny with or without a sale.

Nice game, story is very atmospheric and just right to get dived into. Graphics are great balance between looks and good fps (crucial for my potato gpu), bugs are almost non existent, just few times when you minimalize game to do something other, few buggy/glitchy textures changing black and white colors, but they get fixed by itself. Nice game, on sale for great price defo worth playing.

Given the announcement of a new Metro game, I replayed the older titles. my my oh my, these are dirty, nasty, radiated games, if you have not had the pleasure you will not be disappointed, excellent story telling wrapped perfectly by a world that builds on every part of it.
Some crashes here and there but gameplay and storywise, one of the greatest things I've played.

Metro 2033 is a gripping and intense survival horror experience that will leave you breathless. Set in a post-apocalyptic Moscow, the game takes you on a thrilling journey through the treacherous tunnels of the metro system, where radiation has ravaged the city and mutants lurk in every shadow.

The atmosphere in Metro 2033 is oppressive and foreboding, with the hissing sound of gas masks and the creaking of doors creating a sense of tension that's hard to shake. The game's dark, gritty world is brought to life through stunning visuals and a haunting soundtrack, drawing you into the story of Artyom, a young survivor on a quest to protect his metro from the supposed terror of the Dark Ones.

The gameplay is engaging and challenging, with a perfect balance of stealth, action, and exploration. You'll need to scavenge for supplies, avoid deadly mutants, and make tough decisions to survive in this unforgiving environment.

Metro 2033 is a must-play for fans of survival horror and anyone looking for a gripping narrative-driven gaming experience. With its atmospheric setting, engaging gameplay, and emotional storytelling, it's an adventure you won't soon forget.

7.9 out of 10 (Not sure how it plays in the sneaky mode horror version I did the shooter version.)

Pros: 
- Great Graphics for such a small and older game
- Storyline is decent definitely a twist ending
- Good voice acting if your okay with thick Russian / Ukrainian accents
- I played it on a harder mode accidently but made it more fun and challenging

Cons:
- Short game took about 13 hours
- You can only hold two guns at a time with super low ammo capacities felt like I couldn't really have fun and use many weapons.
- Game lacked depth there was no level up perks or skills you can enhance in anyway

I bought this a while ago on sale and now i have both the Original and 2033 redux on GOG i might 100% those and this one, why? Idk LOL

Fantastic game, i think the redux is better than the original because stealth is actually useable, gameplay is better but the character models in the redux look so much worse.
metro is an amazing series, even for the first installment its a really great game. highly recommend.

7.5/10 for me and 10/10 would recommend. My only issue is that diary pages reset with a new game so my advice is bum rush speed run on ranger to get the bad ending (canon) then play again on survivor but get all the pages and do the side stuff for the good ending. 

Bug wise I encountered crashing in 2 missions being caves, and the final mission. After attaching a debugger apparently the 4a engine error was something about an entity limit. Never figured out what triggered that tbh. Also that crane if super finnicky lol

Overall i'd say its a great game
